nameMeanings.name: Tour
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  African/Muslim Mamadou, Oumar, Abdoulaye, Amadou, Ibrahima, Mohamed, Sekou, Askia, Bakary, Daouda, Moctar, Moussa, Omar, Seydou, Abdullah, Aboubacar, Ahmed, Aisha, Aliou, Aminata, Babacar, Boubacar, Cherif, Elhadji.

West African (mainly Mali, Guinea, Ivory Coast, and Senegal; Touré): from the name of the Touré clan of the Soninke and the closely related Mandinka peoples, which is from a Soninke word meaning ‘elephant’. This surname is also found among some other peoples, e.g. the Tukulors. Compare Touray and Turay .

variants.name: Touray
meanings: West African (mainly Gambia): variant of Touré (see Toure ). Compare Turay .

variants.name: Turay
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  African/Muslim Mohamed, Zainab, Alieu, Ismail, Ahmed, Alimamy, Fatmata, Foday, Hamid, Ibrahim, Idrissa, Isata, Isatu, Kadiatu, Khadijah, Mamadou, Mohammed, Ramatu.
West African (mainly Sierra Leone and Liberia): from a variant of the name of the Touré (see Toure ) clan of the Mandinka and, subsequently, Sierra Leonean Limba and Temne peoples. Compare Touray .
Hungarian: habitational name for someone from a place called Tura.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Deborah
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
